title: cell.fill issue
author: ns20011997

Hi,

comments.author: L-van-der-merwe
comments.body:
> Steps :
> 
> * create a file
> * open it with MS excel
> * select cells A1 to C5 (It's an example)
> * assign a style
> * close the file
> * open it with exceljs
> * run `ws.getCell('B2').font = {...cell.font, color: {argb: 'FF3366FF'}}`
> * style of A1 to C5 will be changed
> 
> ### Workaround
> ```js
> const cell = ws.getCell('B2')
> cell.style = {
>    ...cell.style,
>    font: {...cell.font, color: {argb: 'FF3366FF'}}
> }
> ```
> 
> ### Idea
> May be, create a method `setStyle()` on Cell class.
> 
> ```js
> setStyle(style) {
>   this.style = {...this.style, ...style}
> }
> ```
> 
> #### Use example :
> ```js
> const cell = ws.getCell('B2')
> cell.style = {
>    ...cell.style,
>    font: {...cell.font, color: {argb: 'FF3366FF'}} -- This worked perfectly. Thanks
> }
> ```
> 
> become :
> 
> ```js
> const cell = ws.getCell('B2')
> cell.setStyle({font: {...cell.font, color: {argb: 'FF3366FF'}}})
> ```
> 
> #### Use example :
> ```js
> const cell = ws.getCell('B2')
> cell.style = {
>   ...cell.style,
>   fill: {
>     type: 'pattern',
>     pattern: 'solid',
>     fgColor: {argb: 'FFD8D8D8'}
>   }
> }
> ```
> 
> become :
> 
> ```js
> ws.getCell('B2').setStyle({
>   fill: {
>     type: 'pattern',
>     pattern: 'solid',
>     fgColor: {argb: 'FFD8D8D8'}
>   }
> })
> ```

comments.author: GarryPlays
comments.body:
@L-van-der-merwe THANK YOU!!! I went bonkers over the cell style issue. 
Why is your setStyle not a thing yet? I think your proposal sounds great.
